sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, random_value DOUBLE, created_at DATE);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, random(), DATE '2023-01-01'), (2, random(), DATE '2024-01-01');
SELECT id, random_value, created_at - INTERVAL (CAST(random_value * 10 AS INTEGER)) DAY AS adjusted_date FROM test_table WHERE adjusted_date IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INTEGER, description VARCHAR);
SELECT t.id, a.description, created_at - INTERVAL (SEED(0.1) + 5) DAY AS new_date FROM test_table t JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.ref_id WHERE a.description IS NOT NULL ORDER BY new_date ASC;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, created_at FROM test_table GROUP BY created_at HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT id, SUM(random_value) OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY created_at) AS cumulative_sum FROM test_table WHERE id IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t.id, (created_at - INTERVAL 5 DAY) AS date_subtraction, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM another_table a WHERE a.ref_id = t.id) AS related_count FROM test_table t;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN additional_info VARCHAR;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, random_value, created_at, additional_info) VALUES (4, random(), DATE '2022-02-03', 'New Info');
